metadata.title: Guideline on the Clinical Investigations of Medicinal Products for the Treatment of Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension

rules.rule_text: Pulmonary hypertension is defined by a mean pulmonary artery pressure (PAP) > 25 mmHg at rest. This guideline focuses on Group 1 PAH but can be applied to Group 4 (CTEPH) as well.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is defined by a mean pulmonary artery pressure (PAP) > 25 mmHg at rest.
rules.exceptions: Paediatric PAH addressed in separate addendum

rules.rule_text: All-cause mortality is of special relevance in PAH. Any new drug should at least be shown to have no detrimental effect on survival. All mortality data and their underlying cause must be reported.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any new drug should at least be shown to have no detrimental effect on survival. It is mandatory to report all mortality data and their underlying cause.

rules.rule_text: Specific claims on mortality can only be supported by long-term controlled studies including death as a primary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: Specific claims on mortality can only be supported by long-term controlled studies including death as a primary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Inclusion of mortality endpoint in the investigational plan is strongly encouraged, particularly in patients with severe disease.
rules.verbatim_quote: Still, the inclusion of this endpoint in the investigational plan, in particular in patients with severe disease, is strongly encouraged

rules.rule_text: PAH-related hospitalization must be clearly defined (at least 24 hours, caused by a PAH-related clinical condition such as right heart failure, arrhythmia, syncope, haemoptysis, chest pain, dyspnoea, or catheter implantation for epoprostenol).
rules.verbatim_quote: Hospitalization for PAH should be clearly defined (e.g. for at least 24 hours caused by a clinical condition related to PAH such as right heart failure, arrhythmia, syncope, haemoptysis, chest pain, dyspnoea or hospitalization to implant a catheter to initiate epoprostenol treatment).

rules.rule_text: WHO/NYHA functional classification has prognostic predictive value but its subjectivity precludes use as a sole primary endpoint. It can be used to indicate clinical worsening or as a secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: However, the subjectivity of this functional classification that depends on both the patient and the caregiver, precludes its utility as a sole primary endpoint. Changes in FC can be used to indicate clinical worsening (see 4.2.2) or can be used as a separate secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: The 6-minute walk test (6-MWT) can be used as a primary endpoint when the indication is restricted to improvement in exercise capacity, provided there is no negative impact on survival.
rules.verbatim_quote: When the indication is restricted to an improvement in exercise capacity, the 6- MWT can be used as the primary endpoint, provided there is no negative impact on survival.
rules.exceptions: 6-MWT has limitations; lacks clear correlation between exercise improvement and overall survival

rules.rule_text: The minimal meaningful clinical difference for 6-MWT must be adequately defined a priori, including for non-idiopathic PAH, less severe patients, and combination therapy scenarios.
rules.verbatim_quote: The minimal meaningful clinical differences (clinical impact) in case of non-idiopathic PAH, less severe PAH patients, or in combination therapy should be adequately defined a-priori.

rules.rule_text: A composite primary endpoint of time to clinical worsening is encouraged. Suggested components: all-cause death, time to non-planned PAH-related hospitalization, and time to PAH-related deterioration (increase in WHO FC, deterioration in exercise testing, or signs/symptoms of right-sided heart failure).
rules.verbatim_quote: The investigation of a composite primary endpoint that reflects, in addition to mortality, time to clinical worsening is encouraged. The composition of this composite endpoint may vary depending on the severity and the aetiology of the disease. The following components are suggested: 1. All-cause death. 2. Time to non-planned PAH-related hospitalization. 3. Time to PAH-related deterioration identified by at least one of the following parameters: i. increase in WHO FC; ii. deterioration in exercise testing iii. signs or symptoms of right-sided heart failure
rules.exceptions: Composition may vary based on disease severity and aetiology

rules.rule_text: All chosen parameters for composite endpoints must be clinically relevant, adequately defined, well validated, and centrally adjudicated by a blinded adjudication committee.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any chosen parameter should be clinically relevant, adequately defined, well validated and centrally adjudicated by a blinded adjudication committee.

rules.rule_text: Each individual component of the composite endpoint must be examined separately as secondary endpoints to ensure the effect of one component does not negate the effect of another.
rules.verbatim_quote: The influence of each individual component should also be examined separately as secondary endpoints to ensure that the effect of one component is not negating the effect of another.

rules.rule_text: Haemodynamic measurements (right heart catheterization) are currently limited to diagnosis and as a secondary endpoint. They may play an important role in early development for mechanism of action elucidation and dose-response definition.
rules.verbatim_quote: the value of haemodynamic measurements in the evaluation of medicinal products is not as clear. Thus the place of these measurements is currently limited to the diagnosis and as a secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Quality of life measurements must include patient perception of disease impact on daily life, physical, psychological, and social functioning.
rules.verbatim_quote: Measurements should accordingly include the patient's perception of the impact of his/her disease and its treatment on his/her daily life, physical, psychological and social functioning and well being.

rules.rule_text: Biomarkers (neuro-hormones, natriuretic peptides, endothelin-1, norepinephrine) can currently only be considered supportive for efficacy, not primary evidence.
rules.verbatim_quote: Although the investigation of such biomarkers is encouraged to elucidate the mechanism of disease, their value can currently only be considered as supportive for efficacy.

rules.rule_text: Diagnostic criteria for PAH and its aetiology must be clearly stated. Baseline disease stage must be categorized by NYHA/WHO functional class, exercise testing, and haemodynamic measurements if possible.
rules.verbatim_quote: The criteria for the diagnosis and the aetiology of PAH should be clearly stated. The baseline disease stage categorized mainly by clinical symptoms (NYHA/WHO) (and exercise testing and haemodynamic measurements if possible) should be adequately assessed.

rules.rule_text: Incident vs. prevalent patients and time from diagnosis must be stated to facilitate better characterization and comparison with other drugs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Incident versus prevalent patients, and time from diagnosis should also be stated. These criteria facilitate better characterization of the recruited patients and consequently the gained efficacy potential. This also allows comparison with other drugs.

rules.rule_text: Proper representation of subgroups is necessary if specific claims are made relating to aetiology, functional class, or concomitant PAH therapy. Targeting functional classes beyond WHO II and IV is encouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: Proper representation of the subgroups is necessary if specific claims are made relating to aetiology, FC or concomitant PAH therapy. Targeting other FC is encouraged e.g. WHO II and IV.

rules.rule_text: Background therapy must be clarified and standardized. Empirical use of calcium channel blockers must not be allowed. Patients must be sufficiently stable on background medications before recruitment.
rules.verbatim_quote: The allowed background therapy should be clarified and standardized as much as possible. Empirical use of calcium channel blockers should not be allowed. Patients should be sufficiently stable on their background medications before recruitment in the clinical study.

rules.rule_text: For combination therapy claims, the number of enrolled patients on specific PAH therapy (prostanoids, endothelin blockers, PDE5 inhibitors) must be sufficiently representative. These claims must be pre-specified in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: the number of enrolled patients on a specific PAH-therapy (prostanoids, endothelin blockers or PDEs) should be sufficiently representative to allow corresponding claims of combined therapy. These claims have to be pre-specified in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: PD studies must elaborate mechanism of action and dose-response relationship, including effects on haemodynamic parameters, pulmonary effects, and neurohormonal function. PAH patients should preferably be recruited.
rules.verbatim_quote: The pharmacodynamic studies should elaborate on the mechanism of action and dose-response relationship. The effects on haemodynamic parameters, pulmonary effects and neurohormonal function are important at this stage and should be thoroughly studied, especially for a new class of drugs. ... Preferably, patients with PAH should be recruited for these studies.

rules.rule_text: Interaction studies between the investigational drug and potential background/specific PAH medications must be planned, investigating both PK and PD interactions.
rules.verbatim_quote: Considering the future trend of combination therapy, interaction studies between the investigational drug and that of specific PAH medications should be planned investigating possible both PK and PD interactions.

rules.rule_text: Exploratory studies should investigate at least 2 doses on clinical symptoms and/or haemodynamic responses. For 6-MWT, minimum study duration is 12 weeks.
rules.verbatim_quote: These studies could be placebo-controlled, investigating the efficacy of at least 2 doses on clinical symptoms and/or haemodynamic responses ... In case of the 6-MWT the standard is a minimum duration of 12 weeks.
rules.exceptions: Ethical problems in conducting placebo-controlled studies are acknowledged

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory therapeutic studies must be controlled, double-blind, and randomized. For add-on indications, a placebo group is mandatory. For monotherapy to show exercise improvement, actively-controlled studies should be considered.
rules.verbatim_quote: Controlled double-blind randomized studies are required. If the proposed indication for the investigational drug is add-on to an existing therapy, a placebo-group is mandatory. If the proposed indication is monotherapy to show improvement in exercise capacity, actively-controlled studies should be considered
rules.exceptions: Use of placebo in monotherapy claims for clinical worsening must be justified considering established therapeutic options

rules.rule_text: Studies using exercise testing endpoint may vary between 3-6 months. A minimum of 6 months is usually necessary for time to clinical worsening endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies using improvement in exercise testing may vary between 3 and 6 months. A minimum of 6 months is usually necessary to demonstrate an improvement in time to clinical worsening
rules.exceptions: Depends on composition of endpoints and disease severity

rules.rule_text: Even if a claim of prolonged survival is not made, effect on mortality must be reported. This can be done in an open extension phase.
rules.verbatim_quote: Even if a claim of prolonged survival is not made, effect on mortality should be reported. This can be done in an open extension phase.

rules.rule_text: Groups must be sufficiently balanced with respect to age, sex, aetiology, severity, and background medication.
rules.verbatim_quote: For any proposed indication, provided the study is properly randomized, groups should be sufficiently balanced in respect to age, sex, aetiology, severity and background medication.

rules.rule_text: Long-term safety data collection must continue even after drug registration due to the rarity of the disease and limited safety database. Open extension phases are recommended. Vigilant monitoring of anticipated adverse events based on pharmacological class signals is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Due to the rarity of the disease, the safety database may be quite limited at the time of registration. Every attempt should be made to collect long-term data on adverse events and interactions even after drug registration. Recruitment of the patients enrolled in the controlled studies (exploratory or confirmatory) in open extension phases is recommended. ... Vigilant monitoring of anticipated adverse events based on signals from pre-clinical studies or due to the pharmacological class should be done
